intake.user_request: Fix IMP-CORE-052: Replace regex-based parsing with tree-sitter AST for TypeScript, JavaScript, Go, Rust, Java, C#, and PHP. Python already uses tree-sitter at 100% accuracy. Expanding AST coverage to remaining languages is the highest-ROI scanner quality improvement (estimated accuracy jump from 6.5/10 to 8+/10).

classification.plan_type_rationale: Multi-language AST rollout touching scanner core, pattern-loader, and per-language grammar bindings. Each language is an independent slice but shares the same infrastructure changes. Phased delivery de-risks regression: TypeScript/JavaScript first (highest traffic), then compiled languages (Go, Rust), then JVM/other (Java, C#, PHP). Gate between phases on accuracy regression tests.

hotspot_files.reason: 1500+ lines, 15 language paths, AST + regex modes

in_scope: Phase 1: TypeScript + JavaScript — tree-sitter AST parity with Python path
Phase 2: Go + Rust — tree-sitter grammar bindings + scanner integration
Phase 3: Java + C# + PHP — tree-sitter grammar bindings + scanner integration
Accuracy regression test suite for each language (ground-truth fixtures)
Worker path (scanner-worker.ts) must be updated to use AST path for TS/JS
out_of_scope: Languages not already supported (no new language onboarding)
Data-flow or type-flow analysis (IMP-CORE-053, separate WO)
Python scanner (already AST, no change needed)

constraints: tree-sitter bindings must be available as npm packages (no native compile step added to CI)
Fallback to regex if tree-sitter grammar fails to load — no hard crash on grammar missing
All existing scanner tests must pass after each phase
Performance: AST scan must not exceed 2x sequential regex scan time per file
